executive_summary: Acme Cloud Technologies presents a CRITICAL risk MSA scoring 78/100, driven primarily by a 30-day liability cap, unilateral indemnification, and perpetual data licence granted to the vendor. This contract must not be signed until a minimum of 6 must-change redlines are accepted, beginning with removal of the perpetual data licence and expansion of the liability cap to 12 months of contract value.
walk_away_clauses: Perpetual irrevocable data licence granted to Vendor
Liability cap limited to 30 days fees
Cayman Islands governing law and arbitration

redline_items.original_text: Vendor's total liability shall not exceed fees paid in the prior 30 days.
redline_items.redlined_text: Vendor's total cumulative liability shall not exceed the total fees paid by Customer in the twelve (12) months immediately preceding the event giving rise to the claim.
redline_items.change_rationale: 30-day cap provides virtually no financial protection for enterprise. Industry standard is 12 months of contract value minimum.

redline_items.original_text: Customer grants Vendor a perpetual, irrevocable licence to use anonymised customer data for product improvement.
redline_items.redlined_text: Vendor may use aggregated, anonymised Customer data solely for service improvement purposes during the term of this Agreement. All such rights terminate upon expiry or termination of this Agreement.
redline_items.change_rationale: Perpetual irrevocable licence means Vendor retains data rights forever even after contract ends. This is a walk-away condition.

redline_items.original_text: Customer shall indemnify Vendor against all third-party claims. Vendor indemnification limited to IP infringement only, capped at $10,000.
redline_items.redlined_text: Each party shall indemnify and defend the other against third-party claims arising from its own acts or omissions. In the event of a data breach caused by Vendor, Vendor shall indemnify Customer for all resulting costs, damages, and regulatory fines without cap limitation.
redline_items.change_rationale: Unilateral indemnification with $10,000 cap on vendor side creates massive asymmetric risk. Mutual indemnification is non-negotiable.

redline_items.original_text: Vendor targets 99.0% monthly uptime but provides no warranty. SLA credits limited to 5% of monthly fees.
redline_items.redlined_text: Vendor guarantees 99.9% monthly uptime measured on a rolling 30-day basis excluding scheduled maintenance windows with minimum 48 hours notice. Service credits for failure to meet SLA shall be 10% of monthly fees per 0.1% below the guaranteed threshold, up to 50% of monthly fees.
redline_items.change_rationale: 99.0% allows 7.3 hours downtime per month with virtually no financial consequence. 99.9% is industry standard with meaningful credit structure.

negotiation_summary: Take an aggressive negotiation stance on this contract — four clauses represent walk-away or must-change conditions that significantly favour the vendor. Begin negotiations by presenting the perpetual data licence removal as a non-negotiable condition before discussing any commercial terms.
